title: अम्बा स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीअम्बास्तुतिः ॥

नमो देव्यै महादेव्यै शिवायै सततं नमः।
नमः प्रकृत्यै भद्रायै नियताः प्रणताः स्म ताम्॥१॥

जगदम्बे त्वां प्रणमामि सर्वलोकनिवासिनि।
आदिशक्ते जगन्मातः पाहि मां शरणागतम्॥२॥

त्वं ब्रह्मविद्या विद्यानां महाविद्या महास्मृतिः।
महामेधा महाधीमती महामन्त्रा महाद्युतिः॥३॥

त्वं श्रीस्त्वमीश्वरी त्वं ह्रीस्त्वं बुद्धिर्बोधलक्षणा।
त्वं पुष्टिस्त्वं तुष्टिस्त्वं शान्तिः क्षान्तिरेव च॥४॥

नमोऽम्बे सर्वसंहर्त्रि नमोऽम्बे सर्वदायिनि।
नमोऽम्बे सर्वशक्तिस्थे नमोऽम्बे सर्वमंगले॥५॥

दुर्गे दुर्गतिनाशिन्यै नमो नारायणी शुभे।
नमस्ते गरुडारूढे नमस्ते कोलवाहिनि॥६॥

त्वं वैष्णवी शक्तिरनन्तवीर्या
विश्वस्य बीजं परमासि माया।
सम्मोहितं देवि समस्तमेतत्
त्वं वै प्रसन्ना भुवि मुक्तिहेतुः॥७॥

सर्वभूतेषु या देवी बुद्धिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥८॥

सर्वभूतेषु या देवी शक्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥९॥

सर्वभूतेषु या देवी शान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१०॥

सर्वभूतेषु या देवी श्रद्धारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥११॥

सर्वभूतेषु या देवी लक्ष्मीरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१२॥

माता मे सर्वलोकानां त्वमेव जगदम्बिका।
त्वत्प्रसादेन जीवामि त्वां विना शून्यमेव मे॥१३॥

इति स्तुत्वा महाम्बां ये पठन्ति प्रत्यहं नराः।
तेषां सर्वार्थसिद्धिः स्यात् अम्बा तुष्यति सर्वदा॥

॥ इति श्रीअम्बास्तुतिः सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Amba Stutih ||

Namo Devyai Mahadevyai Shivaayai Satatam Namah.
Namah Prakrityai Bhadraayai Niyataah Pranataah Sma Taam.||1||

Jagadambe Tvaam Pranamami Sarvalokanivaasini.
Aadishakte Jaganmaatah Paahi Maam Sharanagatam.||2||

Tvam Brahmavidyaa Vidyaanaam Mahaavidyaa Mahasmritih.
Mahaamedha Mahaadheematee Mahaamantraa Mahaadyutih.||3||

Tvam Shreestvameeshvaree Tvam Hreest-Tvam Buddhirboddhalakshanaa.
Tvam Pushtistvam Tushtistvam Shaantih Kshaantireva Cha.||4||

Namoambe Sarvasamhartri Namoambe Sarvadaayini.
Namoambe Sarvashaktisthe Namoambe Sarvamangale.||5||

Durge Durgatinashinaayai Namo Naaraayani Shubhe.
Namaste Garudaaruddhe Namaste Kolavahini.||6||

Tvam Vaishhnavee Shaktiranantaveeryaa
Vishvasya Beejam Paramaasi Maayaa.
Sammohitam Devi Samastametat
Tvam Vai Prasannaa Bhuvi Muktihetuh.||7||

Sarvabhooteshu Yaa Devee Buddhiroopena Samsthitaa.
Namastasyai Namastasyai Namastasyai Namo Namah.||8||

Sarvabhooteshu Yaa Devee Shaktiroopena Samsthitaa.
Namastasyai Namastasyai Namastasyai Namo Namah.||9||

Sarvabhooteshu Yaa Devee Shaantiroopena Samsthitaa.
Namastasyai Namastasyai Namastasyai Namo Namah.||10||

Sarvabhooteshu Yaa Devee Shraddharoopena Samsthitaa.
Namastasyai Namastasyai Namastasyai Namo Namah.||11||

Sarvabhooteshu Yaa Devee Lakshmeeroopena Samsthitaa.
Namastasyai Namastasyai Namastasyai Namo Namah.||12||

Maataa Me Sarvalokaanaam Tvameva Jagadambikaa.
Tvatprasaadena Jeevaami Tvaam Vinaa Shoonyameva Me.||13||

Iti Stutvaa Mahaambaam Ye Pathanti Pratyaham Naraah.
Teshaam Sarvaarthasiddhih Syaat Ambaa Tushyati Sarvadaa.

|| Iti Shri Amba Stutih Sampoornaa ||
hindiMeaning:
१. देवी को, महादेवी को, शिवा (कल्याणी) को सतत नमस्कार। प्रकृति को, भद्रा को — हम नियमित प्रणत हैं उन्हें।

२. हे जगदम्बे! सर्वलोकनिवासिनी! आपको प्रणाम करता हूँ। हे आदिशक्ति जगन्मात! शरणागत मेरी रक्षा कीजिये।

३. आप विद्याओं में ब्रह्मविद्या, महाविद्या, महास्मृति, महामेधा, महाधीमती, महामन्त्रा, महाद्युति हैं।

४. आप श्री हैं, ईश्वरी हैं, ह्री हैं, बोधलक्षणा बुद्धि हैं। आप पुष्टि, तुष्टि, शान्ति और क्षान्ति (क्षमा) हैं।

५. हे अम्बे! सर्वसंहर्त्री को नमस्कार। सर्वदायिनी को नमस्कार। सर्वशक्तिस्था को नमस्कार। सर्वमंगला को नमस्कार।

६. हे दुर्गे! दुर्गतिनाशिनी! नारायणी! शुभे! गरुड़ारूढ़ा! कोलवाहिनी (वाराहरूपा)! आपको नमस्कार।

७. आप वैष्णवी शक्ति, अनन्तवीर्या, विश्व का बीज, परमा माया हैं। हे देवी! यह सब सम्मोहित है — आप प्रसन्न होकर भूमि पर मुक्ति का कारण बनती हैं।

८. सर्वभूतों में बुद्धि-रूप से स्थित जो देवी हैं — उन्हें नमस्कार, नमस्कार, नमस्कार, बार-बार नमस्कार।

९. सर्वभूतों में शक्ति-रूप से स्थित देवी को — बार-बार नमस्कार।

१०. सर्वभूतों में शान्ति-रूप से स्थित देवी को — बार-बार नमस्कार।

११. सर्वभूतों में श्रद्धा-रूप से स्थित देवी को — बार-बार नमस्कार।

१२. सर्वभूतों में लक्ष्मी-रूप से स्थित देवी को — बार-बार नमस्कार।

१३. सब लोकों की माता आप ही हैं, जगदम्बिका। आपके प्रसाद से जीवित हूँ — आपके बिना मेरा सब कुछ शून्य है।

जो मनुष्य इस प्रकार महाम्बा की स्तुति कर प्रतिदिन पाठ करते हैं, उन्हें सर्वार्थसिद्धि होती है और अम्बा सर्वदा प्रसन्न रहती हैं।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीअम्बास्तुतिस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य मार्कण्डेय ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् एवं उपजाति छन्दः। श्री अम्बा (दुर्गा) देवता। ॐ ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं बीजम्। अम्बा शक्तिः। सर्वार्थसिद्धि कीलकम्। श्रीअम्बाप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Comprehensive praise of the Divine Mother incorporating key verses from Durga Saptashati
Contains the famous 'Ya Devi Sarvabhuteshu' formula praising Devi in all beings
Recognizes the Mother as Buddhi, Shakti, Shanti, Shraddha, and Lakshmi in all creatures
Invokes fulfillment of all purposes (sarvartha siddhi)
Ideal daily prayer for those devoted to the Divine Mother
Combines Vedic and Puranic praise in accessible form
Expresses complete surrender — 'Without You everything is void'
description: Amba Stuti — अम्बा स्तुति — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Durga. Attributed to Markandeya Purana (Durga Saptashati tradition), this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to combine Vedic and Puranic praise in accessible form, grant fulfillment of all purposes (Sarvartha Siddhi), and recognize the Mother as Buddhi, Shakti, Shanti, Shraddha, and Lakshmi in all creatures. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri, Durga Puja, and Chaitra Navratri. With 13 verses and a reading time of about 6 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Amba Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Amba Stuti in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Thirteen verses of heartfelt praise to the Divine Mother including Ya Devi Sarvabhuteshu mantras.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Amba and what does the Amba Stuti praise?
seoFaqs.answer: Amba — meaning mother in Sanskrit — is one of the most beloved names of the Divine Mother, used across all major Shakta sampradayas to address Goddess Durga, Parvati, Mahalakshmi or the supreme Adi-Shakti. In Maharashtra and Karnataka she is especially associated with the great temples of Mahalakshmi at Kolhapur (also called Amba Bai), Tuljapur Bhavani (also called Tulja Amba), and the Saptashringi Devi (Saptashringi Nivasini Amba). The Amba Stuti is a hymn of praise that addresses the goddess as the cosmic mother, source of all creation, sustainer of the universe, destroyer of evil, and the supreme refuge of all devotees. The hymn typically invokes her in her benevolent maternal aspect rather than her fierce warrior form. (Sources: Hindupedia Devi; Wikipedia Mahalakshmi Kolhapur; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Amba Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Amba Stuti is preserved in classical Shakta tradition associated with the Markandeya Purana — the same puranic source that contains the famous Devi Mahatmyam (Durga Saptashati). Within the broader Devi Mahatmya tradition multiple stutis are sung by various devotees: the gods, sage Medha, King Suratha, the merchant Samadhi and others, each praising the goddess in characteristic devotional language. The Amba Stuti specifically belongs to this tradition of maternal devotional praise. Compilation texts such as Brihat Stotra Ratnakara and regional stotra-pothi prints reproduce the hymn, and it is widely circulated through Stotra Nidhi, Vedadhara, Sanskritdocuments.org and major regional publishers. (Sources: Hindupedia Devi Mahatmyam; Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Amba Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday and Friday are the principal weekdays for any Devi stotra, with Friday especially associated with Lakshmi-Amba traditions. The supreme annual occasions are the two Navaratris — Sharad Navaratri in Ashvin (the most popular nine-night Devi festival) and Chaitra Navaratri in spring — when the Amba Stuti is recited daily for nine nights. Other peak occasions include Vijayadashami, Lalita Panchami, Durga Ashtami, Mahanavami, Akshaya Tritiya, and the regional festivals of Maharashtra such as Tuljapur Bhavani Yatra and Kolhapur Mahalakshmi festivals. Devotees facing fertility issues, family discord, financial hardship or serious illness traditionally recite the Amba Stuti daily as a prayer to the cosmic mother. (Sources: Drikpanchang Navaratri; Hindupedia Devi worship; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Amba Stuti phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources promise that one who recites the Amba Stuti daily with devotion gains the protection and grace of the cosmic mother, freedom from all fears and obstacles, removal of disease and planetary affliction, prosperity and abundance in family life, fertility and the birth of virtuous children, harmony among family members, victory in righteous undertakings, and ultimately moksha through the maternal grace of the goddess. The hymn is particularly recommended for those seeking maternal solace during times of grief, loneliness, illness or anxiety, since it invokes the goddess in her gentlest and most compassionate aspect. Recitation during Navaratri is said to multiply the benefits manifold. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Amba Stuti and Devi Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are stutis — devotional hymns of praise — addressed to the supreme goddess, and they overlap considerably in theological content. The Devi Stuti generally refers to the famous stutis preserved within the Devi Mahatmyam (Durga Saptashati) — particularly the Brahma Stuti, the Sakaladi Stuti, the Shakradi Stuti and the Narayani Stuti — sung by the gods at various points in the narrative. The Amba Stuti specifically invokes the goddess in her maternal Amba aspect, often associated with the great Maharashtra and Karnataka temples of Kolhapur, Tuljapur and Saptashringi. Devotees often recite both — Amba Stuti for daily personal devotion to the maternal aspect, and Devi Stuti during Navaratri and Saptashati paath. (Sources: Hindupedia Devi Mahatmyam; Stotra Nidhi; Wikipedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Amba Stuti be recited by anyone, including children?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As a Pauranika stotra preserved in the open Markandeya Purana tradition, the Amba Stuti is accessible to all devotees regardless of gender, caste or age, and no formal diksha is required for daily recitation. Children across Maharashtra, Karnataka and Gujarat are commonly taught the Amba Stuti as one of their first Devi prayers, often during Navaratri evening puja with the family. Women include it in the daily morning aarti and during Mangala Gauri vrat for the welfare of the household. The only customary requirements are cleanliness of body and place, sitting facing east or north, and offering kumkum, haldi, red flowers (especially hibiscus and rose), and naivedya of sweets before a Devi murti or image. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What offerings traditionally accompany the Amba Stuti recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional Devi offerings used during Amba Stuti recitation are kumkum (red vermillion), haldi (turmeric), red flowers especially hibiscus (japa pushpa) and rose, sandalwood paste, fragrant incense, and a naivedya of sweets such as puran-poli, modak, sheera (semolina pudding), or seasonal fruits and milk. Devotees light a ghee lamp before a Devi murti, image or yantra — most commonly a Mahalakshmi or Bhavani image in Maharashtra — and the recitation is typically followed by aarti, namaskara prostrations and parikrama. Wearing red or yellow clothing during the recitation is considered auspicious. During Navaratri the offerings expand to include the daily-prescribed colour-coded clothing, flowers and bhog as per the regional tradition. (Sources: Drikpanchang Navaratri; Hindupedia Devi puja; Stotra Nidhi.)
